change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Audio tapes, correspondence, journals, poetry, songs, fiction, plays.
did.abstract: Correspondence, journals, poetry, songs, fiction, and plays by David McIntosh. Includes audio tapes of his short opera, Can.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: David Patrick McIntosh was born in Philadelphia and grew up in Omaha, Nebraska, where he attended a Jesuit school. He later moved to Tennessee, where worked with an alternative theater company in Knoxville, The Play Group, from 1974-1979, and then moved to New York City. In New York he wrote and worked both in the theatre and at the New York City Gay and Lesbian Center where he designed an ornamental garden. He died from complications of AIDS in 1989.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, journals, poetry, songs, fiction, and plays by David McIntosh. Includes audio tapes of his short opera, Can.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: David Patrick McIntosh papers, #7562. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Charles Dean retains literary rights until January 1, 2017. Until that time, people may view the collection, but may not publish any individual's name (other than David McIntosh's) seen in the collection without Dean's consent or permission from that individual. Researchers must sign an agreement to this effect.
